name: Kale
summary: A hardy leafy green exceptionally high in vitamin K and in the eye-protecting carotenoids lutein and zeaxanthin.
superfoodReason: One of the most vitamin-K-dense foods, and a top source of lutein and zeaxanthin for eye health.

benefits.claim: Very high in vitamin K for bones and clotting
benefits.mechanism: Kale is one of the richest sources of vitamin K1, which activates proteins that direct calcium into bone and enable normal blood clotting.

citations.title: Vitamin K — NIH Office of Dietary Supplements (Health Professional Fact Sheet)

benefits.mechanism: Kale is loaded with lutein and zeaxanthin, carotenoids that accumulate in the retina's macula to filter blue light and act as antioxidants. Higher intake is linked to slower age-related macular degeneration.

citations.title: Lutein + zeaxanthin and omega-3 fatty acids for age-related macular degeneration: the AREDS2 randomized clinical trial (JAMA 2013)

surprisingFacts.text: The carotenoids in kale are fat-soluble, so a little olive oil or another fat with your greens meaningfully increases how much lutein and zeaxanthin you absorb.
